prompt: /status
expectedOutcome: Replies with system status and admin access marker for admin chats.

prompt: /diagnose
expectedOutcome: Replies with Telegram relay, Spawner providers, selected providers, and diagnose timing.

prompt: /board
expectedOutcome: Replies with Spawner Board buckets and does not include stale running missions.

prompt: /updates minimal
expectedOutcome: Sets mission update verbosity to minimal.

prompt: make mission updates verbose
expectedOutcome: Sets mission update verbosity to verbose without starting a mission.

prompt: show me kanban and canvas links for missions
expectedOutcome: Sets mission link preference to both board and canvas.

prompt: /access status
expectedOutcome: Shows current Spark access level and allowed capabilities.

prompt: /access 1
expectedOutcome: Sets chat-only access; natural missions should not auto-start.

prompt: /access 3
expectedOutcome: Sets agent access; explicit natural missions can start.

prompt: can you help me think through whether we should build a mission control dashboard before we touch the canvas?
expectedOutcome: Discusses/plans. Must not create a Spawner mission or PRD bridge run.

prompt: maybe we should build a tiny kanban app, what would be the best first version?
expectedOutcome: Ideates on v1. Must not launch a mission.

prompt: that sounds good
expectedOutcome: Acknowledges context. Must not infer and launch a mission from low-information agreement.

prompt: what were we going to build again?
expectedOutcome: Uses recent conversation/memory to summarize the project idea. Must not launch.

prompt: remember this: my preferred mission updates are concise and outcome-focused
expectedOutcome: Stores or routes memory directive without noisy no-op acknowledgement.

prompt: what do you remember about how I like mission updates?
expectedOutcome: Answers from memory if bridge is available, otherwise gives actionable fallback.

prompt: open the local spawner ui
expectedOutcome: Replies with local Spawner URL/status. Must not claim browser control from Telegram.

prompt: what localhost app is running?
expectedOutcome: Asks for clarification or suggests /diagnose instead of hallucinating.

prompt: /run say exactly OK
expectedOutcome: Creates Spawner mission, registers relay, replies with mission id, and later relays completion.

prompt: run a mission to say exactly OK
expectedOutcome: Creates a Spawner mission only if access level allows missions.

prompt: kick off a mission that checks whether the kanban board API is healthy and report only the result
expectedOutcome: Starts mission with a precise health-check goal and returns board/canvas links per preference.

prompt: start a spawner task to inspect the mission relay and tell me the good, bad, and ugly
expectedOutcome: Starts mission; provider output should be summarized cleanly without raw JSON or relay plumbing.

prompt: build a quick vanilla JS page at ./spark-live-direct-probe: Files: index.html, app.js. No build step. Shows hello from Telegram.
expectedOutcome: Creates direct build PRD/request without leaking the path into the PRD body.

prompt: build a Mission Control dashboard called Relay Workshop with a kanban board, canvas, Telegram updates, provider result summaries, acceptance checks, task routing, and a persistent project log
expectedOutcome: Uses advanced PRD mode; project name should be Relay Workshop.

prompt: Use advanced PRD mode. Build this at ./spark-live-galaxy-garden: a vanilla-JS app called Spark Galaxy Garden. Files: index.html, styles.css, app.js, README.md. No build step. Users plant seeds, water them, harvest stardust, persist state, and see animated growth stages.
expectedOutcome: Writes PRD to Spawner canvas bridge and auto-runs through canvas.

prompt: please help me design a project called Relay Workshop with kanban and canvas, but do not build yet
expectedOutcome: Plans only. Must not trigger build intent because user explicitly says not to build.

prompt: /mission status spark-not-a-real-id
expectedOutcome: Returns a useful not-found/error message without crashing.

prompt: /mission pause not-spark-id
expectedOutcome: Rejects fake mission id with example usage.

prompt: test the Telegram to Spawner relay and tell me whether kanban updates arrive
expectedOutcome: If recent diagnostic context exists, answers/follows up; otherwise starts/asks for an explicit mission depending access.

prompt: research what people are saying today about AI coding agents and summarize the practical takeaways
expectedOutcome: Requires sufficient access; should not fabricate live web/X results if access is insufficient.

prompt: inspect https://github.com/openai/codex and tell me what matters for our agent workflow
expectedOutcome: Requires agent-level access or asks to raise access instead of pretending to inspect.

prompt: lol nice
expectedOutcome: Short conversational reply. Must not store low-value memory or launch anything.
Graceful acknowledgement. Must not infer mission/build.
